sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Unveiling HBT Financial (HBT) Q4 Outlook: Wall Street Estimates for Key Metrics
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project HBT Financial (HBT) to report Q4 earnings of $0.64 per share, a 3.2% increase year-over-year, with revenues expected to hit $60.7 million, up 2.8%. Key metrics like Net Interest Margin (FTE) are anticipated to rise to 4.2% from 4.0%, while Net Interest Income (FTE) is forecasted at $50.72 million. The company's shares have seen a 2.7% return over the last month, outperforming the S&P 500.

feed.title: Harbor Launches Active Dynamic Multi-Asset ETF: MAPP
feed.summary: Harbor Capital Advisors has introduced the Harbor Multi-Asset Explorer ETF (MAPP), an actively managed multi-asset strategy aiming for long-term total return with downside risk limitation. The ETF invests in a diversified portfolio of liquid and low-cost ETFs across various asset classes, guided by Harbor's Multi-Asset Solutions Team and a proprietary systematic framework that analyzes macro-economic and market-price variables to adapt to different business cycle regimes. MAPP seeks to dynamically shift its portfolio's risk based on economic and market conditions.

feed.title: Sprott Physical Silver Trust updates at-the-market program to $2 billion
feed.summary: Sprott Asset Management LP has increased the at-the-market equity program for Sprott Physical Silver Trust (NYSE:PSLV) to up to $2 billion. The trust, which invests primarily in physical silver bullion, has seen a 204.13% return over the past year and is trading near its 52-week high. Proceeds from the offering will be used to acquire physical silver bullion.

feed.title: Sprott Physical Silver Trust updates at-the-market program to $2 billion
feed.summary: Sprott Asset Management LP has updated the at-the-market equity program for Sprott Physical Silver Trust (NYSE:PSLV), increasing the offering to up to $2 billion of trust units in the United States and Canada. The trust, which invests primarily in physical silver bullion, currently has a market capitalization of $18.06 billion and has delivered a 204.13% return over the past year. Proceeds from the program will be used to acquire physical silver bullion.

feed.title: Safety monitors still tagging along on Tesla Robotaxi rides in Austin
feed.summary: Despite Elon Musk's promises that safety monitors would be removed from Tesla Robotaxis by the end of 2025, many rides in Austin continue to include a human safety monitor. While some truly driverless Tesla vehicles have been spotted, the majority of customer-hailed rides still have a person present, indicating that Tesla has not yet fully achieved its robotaxi goals. This situation contrasts with Waymo, which offers public driverless rides in Austin without safety monitors.

feed.title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) stock climbs as it sets Feb. 18 earnings date — what traders watch next
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) shares climbed by 1.4% to $5,096 after announcing its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings release on February 18. Analysts expect EPS of $48.59 and revenue of $6.12 billion, with investors focusing on management's 2026 travel demand outlook and marketing expenses. Despite a recent slip, the stock outperformed competitors and remains a key indicator of travel industry health.

feed.title: Hobbs Nicholas, evp and coo of Hunt J B, sells $613k in shares
feed.summary: Nicholas Hobbs, EVP and COO of Hunt J.B. Transport Services Inc. (NASDAQ:JBHT), sold 3,038 shares worth $613,581 on January 20, 2026. This transaction occurred while JBHT's stock is trading near its 52-week high, having gained over 38% in the past six months. Following the sale, Hobbs directly owns 90,318 shares and indirectly holds additional shares through a trust and 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Alaska’s oil renaissance has arrived at the doorstep of an Iñupiaq village
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips' proposed CD-8 oil development, located just two miles from the Iñupiaq village of Nuiqsut, is creating significant tensions. While the village corporation, Kuukpik, has historically benefited from oil development, local leaders and residents are concerned about the project's proximity, fearing noise, light pollution, and impacts on subsistence activities. Despite the broader economic benefits of Alaska's oil "renaissance," this proposal highlights the complex relationship between the industry and Indigenous communities.

feed.title: Intel, Intuitive Surgical, P&G and more set to report earnings Thursday
feed.summary: A diverse lineup of companies, including Procter & Gamble, General Electric, Intel, Capital One, and Intuitive Surgical, are scheduled to report their financial results on Thursday. The article provides estimated EPS and revenue for numerous companies reporting both before and after the market opens, spanning various sectors. Investors can use this information to position themselves ahead of these market-moving announcements.

feed.title: The Truth About Fox Corp Class A (FOXA): Hidden Media Power Play or Total Snooze?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Fox Corp Class A (FOXA), evaluating whether it's a strategic investment or an unexciting stock. It discusses FOXA's position in the media landscape, focusing on its stability through news, sports, and broadcast, contrasting it with rapidly growing tech and streaming companies. The piece concludes that FOXA is a value-oriented, cash-generating stock suitable for investors looking beyond trending "hype" investments, appealing to those who believe in the enduring power of legacy media.

feed.title: Sharing a Coke with race fans, IMS makes the switch to Coca-Cola
feed.summary: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway (IMS) has announced a multiyear partnership with Coca-Cola Consolidated, making Coca-Cola the official soft drink and water sponsor. This agreement replaces Pepsi as the official sponsor and will see Coca-Cola participating in key IMS events, including the Indy 500 Red Carpet, Porch Parties, and Bike to the 500. This partnership aims to integrate Coca-Cola into the race day tradition for fans.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) and Sally Beauty (SBH) Upgraded Amid Growth Expectations for 2026
feed.summary: Raymond James analyst Olivia Tong has upgraded Ulta Beauty (ULTA) to Strong Buy and Sally Beauty (SBH) to Outperform, citing strong growth expectations for 2026, driven by restructuring initiatives and strategic partnerships. Ulta's upgrade reflects its success with younger consumers and loyalty programs, while Sally Beauty's is based on store refreshes and delivery partnerships. Price targets for both companies have been raised, reflecting analyst optimism despite broader market challenges in the beauty sector.

feed.title: 2 Energy Stocks to Buy With $1,000 and Hold Forever
feed.summary: This article recommends Brookfield Renewable (BEP, BEPC) and NextEra Energy (NEE) as two energy stocks to buy for $1,000 and hold indefinitely. Both companies are leaders in clean energy, are projected to have strong growth in the coming decades, and offer attractive, growing dividends. The article highlights their potential for high-powered total returns due to their focus on the transition to lower-carbon energy and increasing power demand.

feed.title: Medical Kits Boost Community Disaster Preparedness
feed.summary: A group of medical volunteers, funded by the Greater Laguna Coast Fire Safe Council and Edison International Foundation, are equipping themselves with specialized field medical kits to enhance disaster preparedness. These kits, containing hospital-grade supplies, are designed to stabilize injuries when access to hospitals is delayed or impossible, particularly in areas prone to natural disasters like earthquakes and wildfires. Dr. William Dodge, an emergency physician, leads this initiative, aiming to empower medical professionals to provide immediate care during emergencies.

feed.title: Kaiser Permanente, PG&E donate nearly $1 million to revive Oakland’s police cadet program
feed.summary: Kaiser Permanente and PG&E have co-donated almost $1 million to bring back Oakland's police cadet program, which had previously been dormant. This funding aims to re-establish the program, enhancing the city's police force capabilities. The revival is a significant community effort to support local law enforcement.

feed.title: Medical marketing leaders on how the new Omnicom will reshape the fabled Chicago ad scene
feed.summary: The Omnicom multibillion-dollar takeover of IPG, which dissolved legacy agencies like MullenLowe, FCB, and DDB, has profoundly impacted the Chicago ad scene, eliciting a somber mood among local medical marketing leaders. While some lament the loss of storied heritage and unique agency cultures, others view it as an opportunity for progress and growth for independent agencies. The consolidation also raises concerns about client relationships, talent retention, and the increasing influence of AI on the industry.

feed.title: Barclays Lowers Price Target for EQT (EQT) to $64, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for EQT (EQT) from $67.00 to $64.00, a 4.48% decrease, but maintains an "Overweight" rating. This decision, led by analyst Betty Jiang, indicates a belief in the company's continued market potential despite revised expectations. EQT, a natural gas production company operating in the Appalachian Basin, has seen various analyst updates recently, with the average target price from 24 analysts at $64.58.

feed.title: Distillate Capital Adjusts Holdings, Boosts Stake in Uber (UBER)
feed.summary: Distillate Capital Partners rebalanced its portfolios for Q4 2025, adding new holdings and divesting others within its U.S. Fundamental Stability & Value Composite. Notably, the U.S. FSV Strategy increased its stake in Uber Technologies (UBER), T-Mobile US (TMUS), and Marathon Petroleum (MPC). Uber, a key player in the technology sector with a $175.29 billion market cap, shows robust revenue growth but declining gross margin, yet maintains strong financial health and positive analyst sentiment.

feed.title: Citigroup Issues Positive Forecast for Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) Stock Price
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE: APD) from $245 to $272, maintaining a "neutral" rating and suggesting a 4.8% upside. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts with an average target of $293.80, the company recently reported quarterly EPS of $3.39 and forecasted FY 2026 EPS between $12.85 and $13.15. The stock saw varied price target adjustments from other firms, including a decrease by Argus and Evercore ISI, while Wolfe Research and Sanford C. Bernstein reiterated "outperform" ratings.

feed.title: DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Citigroup Analyst Says
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) to $50 from $47, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting a 16% upside. This comes after other brokerages like KeyCorp, UBS Group, and Royal Bank of Canada also increased their price targets and issued "overweight" or "outperform" ratings. The stock has a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average analyst target of $45.99, following its recent quarterly earnings beat on EPS and a significant $2 billion share repurchase authorization.

feed.title: Michigan affordable housing group taps solar to cut its carbon footprint
feed.summary: Consumers Energy has partnered with Grand Rapids-based ICCF Community Homes to integrate the affordable housing provider into its Renewable Energy Program. This collaboration commits ICCF to matching its energy consumption with Michigan-developed renewable projects, moving towards a carbon-neutral operation. The program now involves over 50 businesses, collectively representing more than 700 megawatts of renewable capacity, equivalent to powering 84,000 homes annually and offsetting emissions from over 190,500 cars.

feed.title: Keysight Powers the Future of E-Mobility with Test Solutions for High-Power and Megawatt Charging
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies has launched two new electric vehicle (EV) charging test solutions designed to support the industry's shift to high-power and megawatt-level charging. These solutions, the SL2600A Megawatt Charging Discovery System and the enhanced SL1047A Scienlab Charging Discovery System – High-Power Series, aim to accelerate development, ensure reliability, and meet evolving global standards for EV charging infrastructure. They offer modularity, scalability, and compliance with various international charging standards, helping manufacturers reduce development risk and time-to-market for next-generation EVs.

feed.title: Cisco Slipped 3% Last Week. Here’s What’s Behind the Move
feed.summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) stock experienced a 3% decline last week, closing near $73, largely due to a lack of new company announcements or earnings updates to stimulate buying interest. Despite the slip, TIKR's valuation model suggests a target price of $95, indicating a 28.9% upside over the next 2.5 years, based on projected revenue growth, operating margins, and an exit P/E multiple. The article attributes the recent dip to profit-taking after a period of steady gains rather than a change in the company's business outlook.

feed.title: Jeffs' Brands Ltd. Expands into North American Defense Sector with Beesense Partnership
feed.summary: Jeffs' Brands Ltd. announced that its subsidiary, KeepZone AI Inc., has entered a non-exclusive reseller agreement with Beesense Sensors Systems Ltd., an Israeli company specializing in multi-sensor technology. This partnership allows KeepZone to distribute Beesense's ISR products in Canada and Mexico, expanding Jeffs' Brands' presence in the North American defense and security sectors. The collaboration aims to leverage AI expertise to enhance security solutions and support the company's growth in the tech-forward homeland security market.

feed.title: UnitedHealth pledges to return Obamacare profits to customers in 2026
feed.summary: UnitedHealth has announced its commitment to return Obamacare profits to its customers starting in 2026. This pledge was reported by Politico as part of their live updates on breaking news. The article is part of Politico's broader coverage of U.S. and global politics and policy.

feed.title: PPL Corporation and Blackstone Position Themselves to Build Power Plants for Data Centers
feed.summary: PPL Corporation is responding to a call from President Trump and Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro to build new dispatchable generation capacity for data centers. The company has formed a joint venture with Blackstone Infrastructure to construct, own, and operate these facilities, aiming to address critical energy needs and mitigate rising electricity prices. PPL also supports proposed Pennsylvania legislation that would facilitate such construction by encouraging independent power producers and allowing regulated utilities like PPL Electric Utilities to build and own generation assets.

feed.title: Target Can’t Shake Acne Products Benzene Contamination Lawsuit
feed.summary: A federal judge has refused to dismiss most of a proposed class action lawsuit against Target Corp., alleging its Up&Up acne treatment products contain high levels of the carcinogen benzene. The court allowed consumer protection, false advertising, and implied warranty claims to proceed, rejecting Target's argument of federal preemption. This means consumers can continue their legal challenge against the retailer regarding the alleged contamination.

feed.title: Dollar Tree (DLTR) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of Dollar Tree (DLTR) fell 2.9% after BNP Paribas Exane downgraded its stock to "Underperform" and significantly lowered its price target from $118 to $87. This negative outlook from the analyst firm reinforced concerns about the company's prospects. The market's reaction also highlights the lasting impact of Dollar Tree's decision to sell its struggling Family Dollar unit, which, despite being a costly experiment, was positively received by investors as it allowed management to focus on more profitable segments.

feed.title: Why Microchip Tech (MCHP) Might be Well Poised for a Surge
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) is showing strong potential for a stock surge due to solid improvements in earnings estimates and positive analyst optimism. The company has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and has seen significant increases in both current-quarter and full-year earnings estimates, with no negative revisions from analysts. This favorable outlook, combined with a recent 12% gain in share price, suggests MCHP could be a good investment.

feed.title: Invesco (IVZ) Stock Trades Up, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of Invesco (IVZ) rose 3.9% after RBC Capital upgraded the company to 'Outperform' and increased its price target, citing improved organic growth outlook, potential margin expansion, and earnings growth from the QQQ ETF re-classification. This positive analyst sentiment follows a recent move by Barclays to also raise its price target on Invesco, alongside the company's strategic deal to reshape its Canadian fund business. Invesco has seen an 8% increase year-to-date and is trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: Why Lucid Stock Is Skyrocketing Today
feed.summary: Lucid (NASDAQ: LCID) stock is surging today after Rockwell Automation announced it will be providing software for Lucid's manufacturing plant in Saudi Arabia. Despite the significant gains, the stock remains down 64% over the last year. Analysts caution that while the software integration could offer benefits, Lucid still faces structural issues like negative gross margins and slow manufacturing scaling.

feed.title: A Look At Edgewise Therapeutics (EWTX) Valuation After Detailed Late Stage Pipeline Milestone Update
feed.summary: Edgewise Therapeutics (EWTX) has updated investors on its 2026 pipeline milestones for muscular dystrophy and cardiovascular programs, including upcoming trial readouts and a potential New Drug Application. The company's stock has seen significant momentum, with a 90-day return of 92.95% and a 3-year return of 160.24%. Despite trading at a P/B of 5.4x, close to its peers but higher than the broader US pharmaceuticals group, the company remains unprofitable with a substantial net loss, suggesting future growth is largely priced in and subject to clinical and funding risks.

feed.title: Invesco ETF Comparisons Put Spotlight On RSP QQQ And Valuation
feed.summary: Invesco's S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) and QQQ Trust are currently being compared with competitor ETFs from Vanguard and iShares, highlighting portfolio construction and sector diversification differences focusing on risk, concentration, and their role in investor portfolios. These comparisons come as Invesco's stock (NYSE:IVZ) shows strong returns over the past year and three years, drawing attention to how its ETF lineup impacts its position in the asset management industry. The article also notes Invesco's stock trading close to analyst targets but assessed as overvalued by Simply Wall St.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of regional banking company Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) jumped 5.7% after reporting fourth-quarter 2025 financial results that surpassed analyst expectations for both profit and revenue. The company posted revenue of $2.16 billion, an increase of 9.2% year-over-year, and adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.13, a 33% increase. This strong performance has led to the stock setting a new 52-week high, with a 6.9% increase since the beginning of the year.

feed.title: A Look At Crown Castle (CCI) Valuation After Dish Default And Contract Termination
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) is under scrutiny after DISH Wireless defaulted on payment obligations, leading to the termination of their wireless infrastructure agreement and a 4.47% share price decline. Despite this, CCI's Price-to-Sales ratio of 5.9x suggests it is undervalued compared to peers and the industry average, and its Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicates an estimated fair value of US$128.04 against its current US$86.81. Investors are encouraged to consider the contract concentration risks and recent revenue contraction when evaluating the stock.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland stock hits 52-week high at 66.64 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $66.64, marking a 31.77% increase over the past year due to strategic initiatives and strong financial performance. While ADM surpassed Q3 2025 EPS expectations, it faced a revenue shortfall and lowered its full-year EPS guidance. Analyst ratings are mixed, with Morgan Stanley downgrading to Underweight and UBS reiterating a Buy rating, amidst ongoing dividend payments and potential impacts from biofuel policy changes.

feed.title: Hershey’s Undergoes A Brand Update As It Rethinks Paid, Earned And Owned Media
feed.summary: Hershey's is launching its first major brand marketing campaign since 2018, increasing its brand budget by 20% in 2026. This extensive refresh is driven by strategic sponsorships like the US Olympic team and the company's annual NCAA March Madness involvement, alongside the upcoming 250-year anniversary of the United States and the release of a Hershey-produced film. The campaign emphasizes an interconnected approach to paid, earned, and owned media, integrating content creation, retail media expansion, and utilizing retail locations for promotional advantages.

feed.title: Will Expedia (EXPE) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Expedia (EXPE) is positioned for another earnings beat in its upcoming quarterly report, thanks to a history of outperforming estimates and a positive Zacks Earnings ESP. The company has surpassed earnings expectations in its last two quarters, with an average surprise of 3.70%. This, combined with a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), suggests a high probability of exceeding analyst predictions for its next report due February 12, 2026.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland stock hits 52-week high at 66.64 USD
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) stock recently reached a 52-week high of $66.64, marking a 31.77% increase over the past year. Despite this growth, the company faces challenges, including a revenue shortfall in Q3 2025 and a lowered full-year EPS guidance, leading to a downgrade from Morgan Stanley. However, UBS maintains a Buy rating, and ADM continues its 94-year streak of dividend payments.

feed.title: Where Will Eaton (ETN) Stock Be in 1 Year?
feed.summary: Eaton's stock has recently underperformed due to decelerating sales growth and headwinds in its cyclical automotive segments, despite strong performance in aerospace and data centers. The company is poised for a rebound, with electrical and aerospace segments expanding and strategic acquisitions like Boyd Thermal supporting long-term growth. Analysts project a 12% rise in EPS for 2025 and 2026, suggesting its stock could reach $381 within the next year, potentially outperforming the S&P 500.

feed.title: Will Altria (MO) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Altria (MO) is expected to continue its earnings-beat streak in its upcoming report, driven by a positive Zacks Earnings ESP of +3.97% and a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). The company has consistently topped earnings estimates in the past, with an average surprise of 2.90% over the last two quarters. This combination suggests a high probability of another positive surprise when its earnings are released around January 29, 2026.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Buying VeriSign (VRSN) After Its 1-Year Rally?
feed.summary: VeriSign (VRSN) has seen an 18.9% increase in its share price over the past year. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock might be overvalued by 33.9%, estimating an intrinsic value of US$185.34 per share compared to its current price of US$248.12. However, its P/E ratio of 28.36x is very close to its proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 28.24x, suggesting it's trading at a reasonable price based on earnings.

feed.title: Western Digital stock jumps nearly 7% as Wall Street lifts targets ahead of earnings, Innovation Day
feed.summary: Western Digital shares climbed nearly 7% following multiple Wall Street analysts, including Citi, UBS, and Bank of America, raising their price targets due to strong cloud demand and tight drive capacity. Investors are keenly awaiting the company's upcoming earnings report on January 29 and its Innovation Day on February 3, where new AI-driven storage technologies will be showcased. Despite the positive momentum, analysts note that any dip in cloud orders or falling short of margin forecasts could impact the stock.

feed.title: M&T Bank (MTB) Valuation Check After Earnings Growth Buyback Completion And Higher Net Charge Offs
feed.summary: M&T Bank (MTB) has released its 2025 results, reporting increased net income and EPS, a completed share buyback, and higher quarterly net charge-offs. Despite a P/E of 11.9x suggesting undervaluation compared to peers and its fair ratio, a DCF model indicates an even stronger undervaluation with an estimated fair value of US$391.22 against the current US$211.71. Investors should consider the potential impact of higher net charge-offs and reliance on revenue stability on the valuation.

feed.title: Executive VP Alain Bellemare Sold A Bunch Of Shares In Delta Air Lines
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines Executive VP Alain Bellemare recently sold US$2.5m worth of shares at an average price of US$69.21, decreasing his holding by 27%. This sale, along with another earlier sale at a lower price, suggests a negative sentiment from insiders. Despite significant insider selling overall, the company's profitability and growth temper these concerns, though insider transactions don't inspire confidence.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group to Participate at the UBS Financial Services Conference
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group, Inc. announced that Don McCree and Ted Swimmer will participate in the UBS Financial Services Conference on February 10, 2026. A live webcast of their participation will be available on the bank's investor relations website. The company is one of the nation's largest financial institutions, offering a wide range of banking services.

feed.title: Brown & Brown (BRO) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Brown & Brown (BRO) has seen its share price decline by approximately 24.7% over the past year, trading at about US$79. While its P/E ratio of 27.3x appears higher than the industry average, suggesting it might be overvalued by this metric (compared to an estimated fair ratio of 16.4x), a discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates the stock is undervalued with a fair value estimate of US$146.10. The article encourages investors to weigh these differing valuation signals and consider other factors like risks and growth opportunities in their own research.

feed.title: CVS reverses course on closing Van Ness pharmacy in San Francisco
feed.summary: CVS has unexpectedly decided to keep its pharmacy at 701 Van Ness Ave in San Francisco open, reversing an earlier decision to close the location in February. This move ensures the continuation of one of the few CVS locations in that part of the city.

feed.title: PayPal vs. Upstart: Which Fintech Stock is the Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article provides a comparative analysis of two prominent fintech stocks, PayPal and Upstart, evaluating their growth strategies, financial performance, and market challenges. PayPal is highlighted for its robust payment ecosystem, Venmo's expansion, and strategic AI partnerships, while Upstart is noted for its AI-driven lending platform, rapid growth in originations, and diversification into new credit categories. The author concludes that while both have unique strengths, Upstart offers greater near-term upside potential for long-term investors seeking innovation-driven growth, despite its higher volatility and credit sensitivity.

feed.title: Traders Buy Large Volume of Call Options on Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX)
feed.summary: Traders bought a significantly high volume of call options for Novavax (NVAX), indicating short-term bullish sentiment, following news of a non-exclusive license agreement with Pfizer. Despite this positive development and some reiterated "buy" ratings, analysts remain divided on NVAX's outlook, with concerns about execution risks and recent revenue declines tempering optimism. Hedge funds are also split, with several increasing their stakes while others maintain a cautious stance.
